metadata.scope: Maryland Catholic families in Kentucky 1785-1900, focusing on Mattingly family

primary_sources.author: Webb, Benedict Joseph
primary_sources.title: The Centenary of Catholicity in Kentucky

primary_sources.author: Spalding, M.J. (Martin John)
primary_sources.title: Sketches of the Early Catholic Missions of Kentucky

migration_event.origin: St. Mary's County, Maryland (also Charles County, Prince George's County)
migration_event.route: Overland to Pittsburgh, then flatboats down Ohio River to Maysville (Limestone), KY, then inland to Goodwin's Station, Boston, Nelson County
migration_event.destination: Pottinger's Creek, Washington County, KY (10-15 miles from Bardstown)
migration_event.source_quote: In the year 1785, a league of sixty families was formed in Maryland — all Catholics, and mostly residents of St. Mary's county — each one of whom was pledged to emigrate to Kentucky within a specified time. Of the sixty families subscribing to the compact, twenty-five left Maryland early in 1785, and reached Kentucky before the end of spring of the same year.

pottingers_creek_families_1785_to_1800.notes: Webb states these names are 'reasonably certain' for Pottinger's Creek up to 1800; the first names are 'thought to be, in part, those of the twenty-five families of the Maryland league'

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Philip Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Listed in Webb's Pottinger's Creek family roster; separately identified as Washington County Mattingly branch

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: William Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: First of three brothers to arrive. First wife: Miss Spalding (MD); 3 sons James, Edward, Richard. Second wife: Elizabeth Clark; 10 children: William, Mary, Benjamin, Felix, Ignatius, Julia, George, Susan, Catherine, John

children_second_wife.name: George Mattingly
children_second_wife.notes: daughter Sister Mary Charles of Loretto (deceased 1879)

children_second_wife.name: John Mattingly
children_second_wife.notes: Living 1879, St. Charles congregation; daughter in Sisterhood of St. Francis, Shelbyville KY

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Leonard Mattingly Sr.
mattingly_individuals_documented.born: Maryland (St. Mary's County), before ~1745 (past middle life at 1786 arrival)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Patriarch of KY Mattingly family. All 10 children born in Maryland. ~300 living descendants at death in 1827

children.name: Leonard Mattingly Jr.
children.notes: Eldest son; direct ancestor in Spence lineage

children.name: Basil Mattingly
children.notes: Children with Polly: Mahala (married Washington Mattingly), Edward H., Nancy (married A.J. Mudd), Mary Jane (married Joseph Spalding), Henry (married Susan Jane Spalding), Burrilla M. (married J.W. Montgomery)

children.name: John Mattingly
children.notes: Son Benjamin F. Mattingly had distillery interests Louisville 1879

children.name: William Mattingly
children.notes: Son: Dr. C.P. Mattingly of Bardstown

children.name: Joseph Mattingly
children.notes: Mary was sister of Rev. George Hayden who died in Texas ~1840s

children.name: Jane Mattingly
children.notes: Progenitor of all Russells at St. Charles; grandfather of Rev. David Russell, Diocese of Louisville

children.name: Margaret Mattingly
children.notes: Founding member of Sisterhood of Loretto (1812)

children.name: Nancy Mattingly
children.notes: Invalid; wore Loretto habit at home

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Lucas Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.county: Washington County, KY (area of Manton/Holy Rosary)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Third brother; Webb states he learned 'absolutely nothing' about Lucas beyond that descendant John G. Mattingly lived near Manton, Washington County

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Richard Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Separate branch from the three Hardin's Creek brothers. Not part of the 1785 cohort. House served as church station until ~1818.

children.name: John Mattingly
children.notes: First marriage certified by Catholic clergy in Breckinridge County records; married Sarah Wheatley, Feb 18, 1805 by Father Badin

children.name: Zachariah Mattingly
children.notes: First resident Catholic of Hardinsburg; likely donated land for St. Romuald church

mattingly_individuals_documented.descendants_1884: Twenty families of Richard Mattingly's posterity in lower Breckinridge County as of 1884

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Barton Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Came from Maryland with Elias Rhodes in 1795; separate from both the 1785/1786 cohort and Richard Mattingly

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Ignatius Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Listed as town resident of Bardstown ~1836; likely son of William Mattingly (first of three brothers) and second wife Elizabeth Clark, still living in Bardstown 1879

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Ignatius Mattingly (Breckinridge)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Assisted building first log chapel at Hardinsburg 1810; got out heavy timbers and hand-sawed planks for flooring

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Joseph Mattingly (Breckinridge)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Assisted building first log chapel at Hardinsburg 1810 alongside Ignatius Mattingly

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Joseph Mattingly (Washington County)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Listed in founding congregation of St. Rose / St. Ann church, Washington County

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Generose Mattingly (Sister)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Mother Superior, Sisterhood of Loretto, 1842-43. Birth name not given. Daughter of Basil Mattingly and Monica Miles (child: Martha = Sister Generose per Webb footnote p.47)

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Ann Joseph Mattingly (Sister)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Mother Superior, Sisterhood of Loretto, 1882

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Margaret Mattingly (Sister, founding Loretto)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Daughter of Leonard Mattingly Sr. One of the founding band of Christian maidens from which grew the Sisterhood of Loretto (1812). Described by Webb as 'of the band of Christian maidens out of which grew the now well-known Sisterhood of Loretto'

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Edward H. Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: 'An intelligent and highly respected farmer, still living in the neighborhood of his mother's former residence' as of Webb's writing (~1884). Married Alethair, daughter of Thomas Spalding (uncle of Archbishop Spalding of Baltimore). Source of the Father Badin anecdote about visiting the Hagan homestead.

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: John G. Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.parents: Benjamin F. Mattingly and Susan Mary Graves (grandson of Leonard Sr. via John)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Living 1879 near St. Charles Church; large distillery interests in Louisville

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Benjamin F. Mattingly Jr.
mattingly_individuals_documented.parents: Benjamin F. Mattingly and Susan Mary Graves (grandson of Leonard Sr. via John)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Living 1879 near St. Charles Church; large distillery interests in Louisville. Consistent with founding of Mattingly & Moore Distillery in Bardstown 1877.
mattingly_individuals_documented.distillery_connection: PROBABLE — consistent with Webb 1884 description of distillery interests; 1877 founding date and B.F. Mattingly founder name match; confirmation from primary corporate record not found in this research session
mattingly_individuals_documented.confidence: CONFIRMED for distillery interests; PROBABLE for Mattingly & Moore 1877 specifically

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Austin Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Only confirmed out-of-state migrant among the children of Leonard Sr.'s sons

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: George Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Moved within KY but away from Marion County

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: John J. Mattingly (married into)
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Married Mary (daughter of Clement Hill). Living at Florissant, Missouri 1879 with large family. This is a Mattingly marrying into the Hill family, not a descendant of Leonard Sr.'s direct line.

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Washington Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Married Mahala Mattingly (daughter of Basil and Polly). A Mattingly marrying a Mattingly within the community.

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Dr. C.P. Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.parents: William Mattingly (son of Leonard Sr.) and Henrietta Buckman
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Physician in Bardstown as of 1884

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Dr. Ernest Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.parents: grandson of Basil and Polly (Hagan) Mattingly
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Well-known physician of Lebanon, Kentucky as of ~1884

mattingly_individuals_documented.name: Leonard Mattingly Jr.
mattingly_individuals_documented.notes: Webb cites 'a great-grandson of Leonard Mattingly' as informant for family details, and confirms Leonard Jr. married a Cissell. This is the direct ancestor in the Spence family tree. Per prior research (WikiTree-210), his confirmed birth year is 1764 (Maryland) and he had four confirmed Kentucky-born children.

parishes.name: Holy Cross
parishes.notes: First Catholic church built in Kentucky. Ground deeded by Basil Hayden May 1, 1798 to trustees Bald, Cissell, Payne, Brewer.

parishes.name: St. Charles
parishes.notes: Hundreds of Mattingly family descendants in this congregation by 1884 (Webb). The Mattingly heartland.
parishes.mattingly_connection: William, Leonard, Lucas Mattingly founding families (CONFIRMED)

parishes.name: Holy Rosary
parishes.mattingly_connection: John G. Mattingly (Lucas Mattingly branch, CONFIRMED)

parishes.name: St. Romuald
parishes.mattingly_connection: Richard Mattingly family; Zachariah Mattingly donated land; Ignatius and Joseph Mattingly built timbers (CONFIRMED)

parishes.name: St. Joseph
parishes.notes: Cathedral (first cathedral west of the Alleghenies) dedicated 1816. Ignatius Mattingly resident of Bardstown.
parishes.mattingly_connection: Ignatius Mattingly; Ann Joseph Mattingly (Mother Superior Loretto 1882) (CONFIRMED)

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: Sister Margaret Mattingly / founding Loretto
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Daughter of Leonard Sr.; one of the founding band of the Sisters of Loretto (1812), the first religious order of women founded west of the Alleghenies

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: Sister Generose Mattingly (Martha)
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Daughter of Basil and Monica Miles Mattingly; Mother Superior of Loretto 1842-43

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: B.F. Mattingly Jr.
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Founded (PROBABLE) Mattingly & Moore Distillery, Bardstown, 1877; distillery interests in Louisville 1879 per Webb

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: Dr. Ernest Mattingly
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Prominent physician, Lebanon, KY; grandson of Basil and Polly Hagan Mattingly

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: Dr. C.P. Mattingly
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Physician in Bardstown; son of William (Leonard Sr.'s son) and Henrietta Buckman

notable_descendants_and_connections.name: Rev. David Russell
notable_descendants_and_connections.significance: Priest of Diocese of Louisville; great-grandson of Leonard Mattingly Sr. via daughter Jane and her husband Charles Russell

corrections_to_task_brief.finding: The 1785 migration was indeed approximately 25 families (Webb says 'twenty-five left Maryland early in 1785'). Confirmed.
corrections_to_task_brief.note: The Mattingly brothers specifically arrived 'early in 1786' in Hardin's Creek, not with the 1785 Pottinger's Creek cohort, though they were part of the broader 60-family league

corrections_to_task_brief.claim_in_brief: Leonard Mattingly Jr (1764-1843) was part of the 1785 cohort
corrections_to_task_brief.finding: Leonard Jr. came with his father Leonard Sr. who arrived 'early in 1786' in Hardin's Creek. Leonard Jr. was born 1764 in Maryland, so he was ~22 when the family arrived in 1786. He was NOT in the 1785 Pottinger's Creek cohort.
corrections_to_task_brief.note: He is part of the broader 1785 league/migration, but his personal arrival year is 1786

corrections_to_task_brief.finding: Neither Webb (1884) nor Spalding (1844) use this phrase. It is a modern popular designation for the Marion/Washington/Nelson County Catholic settlement area.
